species.name: Stunky
species.description: The poison that gushes from its aft end is accompanied by an utterly evil-smelling odor with such potency that one whiff can induce memory loss.

species.name: Skuntank
species.description: Sprays a poisonous fluid to take down prey. Sometimes, unable to stomach the stench of its own fluid, it leaves the bested prey uneaten.

species.name: Bronzor
species.description: Floats using a mysterious energy. The pattern engraved upon its back is held as sacred and can sometimes be found in imagery from ancient cemeteries and other such timeworn places.

species.name: Bronzong
species.description: Some believe that its bell-like cry opens holes to another world. It has been revered as a deity since ancient times.

species.name: Bonsly
species.description: Its tears elicit sympathy from those who see them, but do not be deceived! This expulsion of body water is merely a physiological mechanism for keeping itself in good health.

species.name: Mime Jr.
species.description: Known to turn up in bustling marketplaces now and again. It mimics people much as a child would, then watches how they react, eyes sparkling.

species.name: Happiny
species.description: In imitation of Chansey, it keeps a round stone tucked into its belly pouch and cherishes it dearly. It gets along well with children and will sometimes play house with them for fun.

species.name: Chatot
species.description: A versatile performer skilled in the imitation of human speech. It is said that older, more experienced Chatot can even understand the meaning of the words they mimic.

species.name: Spiritomb
species.description: It lays curses by thinking wicked thoughts. Writings tell that this Pokémon was born out of the assembly of five score and eight malevolent spirits.

species.name: Gible
species.description: It nests in caves untouched by sunlight. Its sharp teeth may fall out when worn away or after an impact, but they regrow within a few days.

species.name: Gabite
species.description: Though Gabite are usually of a violent disposition, when I gave one a glass bead it had been eyeing covetously, it suddenly became quite docile.

species.name: Garchomp
species.description: Soars across the heavens at blinding speed—a magnificent sight! It has a feral disposition. Utmost caution is required if one meets a Garchomp out in the wilds.

species.name: Munchlax
species.description: Its robust stomach allows it to nonchalantly devour even rotted matter. It pays frequent visits to villages, seeking out food scraps intended for compost.

species.name: Riolu
species.description: Though infantile in appearance, it has the mysterious ability to read the minds of humans. The pure of heart are met with Riolu's approval, while those of ill nature earn only its loathing.

species.name: Lucario
species.description: A most gallant-looking creature. It emits energy waves and controls them with precision, using them to sense even faraway beings. I have given the name ”aura” to this power.

species.name: Hippopotas
species.description: Though large and languid, Hippopotas is difficult to detect due to its tendency to burrow into and lurk beneath the soil. When agitated or excited, it expels sand from its nostrils.

species.name: Hippowdon
species.description: Short-tempered and easily moved to violence. It whips up whirlwinds of sand to crush its foes' spirits, then goes in for the attack.

species.name: Skorupi
species.description: Its claws are not only razor-sharp but poisonous, making Skorupi a highly dangerous Pokémon. It seems to be weakened by cold temperatures, however.

species.name: Drapion
species.description: Has a brutish, ferocious temperament. With immense strength and a sturdy shell off which swords will bounce, it rampages about and wreaks havoc.

species.name: Croagunk
species.description: A poison wielder with a dastardly personality. Despite such qualities, this species is afforded a measure of popularity due to its peculiar cry and comical features.

species.name: Toxicroak
species.description: Its crimson claws contain a virulent toxin. This toxin can be made into a tonic by diluting it, mixing it with several types of wild grass, and boiling it down over two days.

species.name: Carnivine
species.description: Though this is a plant Pokémon, it has a gluttonous and unruly temperament. Carnivine attacks its prey with its cavernous maw wide open.

species.name: Finneon
species.description: What a gorgeous sight this Pokémon is as it swims with its long, pink-painted caudal fins fluttering behind it. Finneon's beautiful appearance has led to its nickname: ”finery fish.”

species.name: Lumineon
species.description: Uses its gleaming fins to hunt its prey. The view of Lumineon schooling near the surface of the sea at night is breathtaking— it's as though there were shining stars right there.

species.name: Mantyke
species.description: Though ball-like in shape, this Pokémon is a proficient swimmer. I have discovered that if a Mantyke spends much time with schools of Remoraid, it will eventually achieve evolution.

species.name: Snover
species.description: One is likely to encounter this Pokémon while out in the snow. There are stories of Snover appearing in human settlements but doing no harm—rather, they bond with the children.

species.name: Abomasnow
species.description: A powerful Pokémon that can split huge boulders with ease. Dislikes associating with others and chooses to live quietly deep within the mountains, playing with the snow.

species.name: Weavile
species.description: This species corners prey as a pack, under the guidance of a leader. Weavile displays increased cunning, leading me to speculate that its evolution caused further brain development.

species.name: Magnezone
species.description: I theorize that a special magnetic field influenced this Pokémon, changing its molecular structure and causing it to evolve. It emits strange radio waves toward space from its antenna.

species.name: Lickilicky
species.description: Its tongue can extend and contract freely, and it is capable of reaching lengths over 10 times Lickilicky's height. Beware of the saliva, as it contains corrosive elements.

species.name: Rhyperior
species.description: This Pokémon evolved through use of a curious item. Its rocklike hide is composed of a mysterious substance and can withstand a blow from a masterwork sword with nary a scratch.

species.name: Tangrowth
species.description: Draped with long vines, it resembles a shrub in appearance. It swings bundles of vines as though they were arms, wrapping them around prey to ensnare them.

species.name: Electivire
species.description: Its evolution was induced by an unusual item, and its electrical output rises along with its heart rate. From its tails, it can unleash an electric current measuring 20,000 volts.

species.name: Magmortar
species.description: Use of a strange item caused this Pokémon to evolve. Fireballs launched from the ends of its tubelike arms are hot enough to melt an iron pot in an instant.

species.name: Togekiss
species.description: Scant few have ever sighted this Pokémon. After studying what literature remains, I am certain Togekiss will reveal itself when peace reigns in the land.

species.name: Yanmega
species.description: Extremely violent. When hunting, it wastes none of its energy, aiming only for prey's most vulnerable spots. Any who manage to tame this Pokémon must be of incredible bravery.

species.name: Leafeon
species.description: Cells similar to those of plants have been found in its fur. Its hard tail can fell a large tree with one stroke, and the tail's sharpness exceeds even that of a sword crafted by a master.

species.name: Glaceon
species.description: Glaceon is able to lower its body temperature very quickly. It freezes the atmosphere, creating diamond dust that glitters like gems while it flutters and dances around.

species.name: Gliscor
species.description: It glides soundlessly on pitch-black wings and sinks sharp fangs into the throat of its prey. It takes on a look of satisfaction once it has entirely drained its prey of blood.

species.name: Mamoswine
species.description: This species reached its zenith during the period known as the ice age. I suspect that Hisui's frigid climate is in harmony with Mamoswine's constitution, thus awakening hidden potential.

species.name: Porygon-Z
species.description: A curious item induced this evolution. The Pokémon's offensive capabilities have greatly increased, but the strangeness of its behavior has magnified in equal measure. This worries me.

species.name: Gallade
species.description: The blades extending from its elbows are sharper than the finest swords. Its swordsmanship, albeit self-taught, is astonishingly impressive.

species.name: Probopass
species.description: It is able to emit powerful magnetism, allowing it control over the iron sand that forms its luscious mustache. Using this iron sand, Probopass forms hard stones with which it smites its prey.

species.name: Dusknoir
species.description: Comes to those whose lives have come to an end and escorts their souls to the afterlife. Known to mistakenly take the souls of those who yet have life left in them, albeit rarely.

species.name: Froslass
species.description: A Pokémon inhabited by the soul of a woman who died bearing a grudge in the snowy mountains. Legends of Froslass placing deathly curses on misbehaving men send shivers down my spine.

species.name: Rotom
species.description: This bizarre Pokémon appears to be a will-o'-the-wisp powered by electricity. Be wary, as Rotom is both smart and mischievous.

species.name: Uxie
species.description: A Pokémon feared but also respected for stealing away the memories of evildoers. I have found records that suggest Uxie holds dominion over knowledge.

species.name: Mesprit
species.description: Known as the Being of Emotion. In legend, this Pokémon was feared, as any who showed disrespect would have their emotions thrown into disarray.

species.name: Azelf
species.description: The dreaded Being of Willpower. Legends tell of this Pokémon manipulating the will of its adversaries and turning them into puppets of its own.

species.name: Dialga
species.description: This Pokémon is revered as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Dialga was what caused the vast river of time to begin flowing in our world.

species.name: Palkia
species.description: This Pokémon is feared as a deity in Hisuian legend. The birth of Palkia was what caused the walls of our world to disappear, creating a sky that spans for infinity.

species.name: Heatran
species.description: Stories tell of this Pokémon being birthed from the boiling magma within Mount Coronet. Its molten-steel body holds many mysteries.

species.name: Regigigas
species.description: According to legend, Regigigas pulled landmasses together and bound them with rope to create the continent of Hisui. Though I have my doubts, the story could well contain a shred of truth.

species.name: Giratina
species.description: There is one Hisuian verse that tells of a powerful light creating a deep shadow. I imagine that this deep shadow is Giratina.

species.name: Cresselia
species.description: Cresselia is reminiscent of the crescent moon. It leaves a brilliant line of light in its wake as it flies across the night sky. I daresay it resembles the heavenly maiden who created the Milky Way.

species.name: Phione
species.description: Can be seen floating offshore during seasons when the seas are warm. Its azure body blends in with the ocean waters—logic suggests this is a defense mechanism against natural predators.

species.name: Manaphy
species.description: Rumored to migrate across the oceans and visit Hisui's coastal waters only rarely. Although Manaphy resembles Phione, it is also quite different. The relation between the two is unclear.

species.name: Darkrai
species.description: On a moonless night, a strange incident occurred in which every one of a village's inhabitants suffered nightmares. The villagers attested that Darkrai appeared before them in these nightmares.

species.name: Shaymin
species.description: When the turning of seasons brings the cruel winter to its end and the joyous people give thanks to the heavens, Shaymin appears and covers the withered land with flowers.

species.name: Arceus
species.description: It is the heavenly fount from which pours the light that shines across Hisui. Its luminance guides and protects all Pokémon. Hisuian mythology states that Arceus is the creator of all things.

species.name: Victini
species.description: When it shares the infinite energy it creates, that being’s entire body will be overflowing with power.

species.name: Snivy
species.description: They photosynthesize by bathing their tails in sunlight. When they are not feeling well, their tails droop.

species.name: Servine
species.description: When it gets dirty, its leaves can’t be used in photosynthesis, so it always keeps itself clean.

species.name: Serperior
species.description: It can stop its opponents’ movements with just a glare. It takes in solar energy and boosts it internally.

species.name: Tepig
species.description: It loves to eat roasted berries, but sometimes it gets too excited and burns them to a crisp.

species.name: Pignite
species.description: When its internal fire flares up, its movements grow sharper and faster. When in trouble, it emits smoke.

species.name: Emboar
species.description: It has mastered fast and powerful fighting moves. It grows a beard of fire.

species.name: Oshawott
species.description: This Pokémon from the Unova region uses the shell on its belly as a weapon to cut down its foes. Thus, I've conferred upon this shell the name ”scalchop”.

species.name: Dewott
species.description: Its exquisite double-scalchop technique is likely the result of daily training, and it can send even masters of the blade fleeing in defeat.

species.name: Samurott
species.description: Hard of heart and deft of blade, this rare form of Samurott is a product of the Pokémon's evolution in the region of Hisui. Its turbulent blows crash into foes like ceaseless pounding waves.

species.name: Patrat
species.description: Extremely cautious, one of them will always be on the lookout, but it won’t notice a foe coming from behind.

species.name: Watchog
species.description: When they see an enemy, their tails stand high, and they spit the seeds of berries stored in their cheek pouches.

species.name: Lillipup
species.description: This Pokémon is far brighter than the average child, and Lillipup won’t forget the love it receives or any abuse it suffers.

species.name: Herdier
species.description: The black fur that covers this Pokémon’s body is dense and springy. Even sharp fangs bounce right off.

species.name: Stoutland
species.description: Stoutland is immensely proud of its impressive moustache. It’s said that moustache length is what determines social standing among this species.

species.name: Purrloin
species.description: Opponents that get drawn in by its adorable behavior come away with stinging scratches from its claws and stinging pride from its laughter.

species.name: Liepard
species.description: This stealthy Pokémon sneaks up behind prey without making any sound at all. It competes with Thievul for territory.

species.name: Pansage
species.description: It’s good at finding berries and gathers them from all over. It’s kind enough to share them with friends.

species.name: Simisage
species.description: Ill tempered, it fights by swinging its barbed tail around wildly. The leaf growing on its head is very bitter.

species.name: Pansear
species.description: This Pokémon lives in caves in volcanoes. The fire within the tuft on its head can reach 600 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Simisear
species.description: When it gets excited, embers rise from its head and tail and it gets hot. For some reason, it loves sweets.

species.name: Panpour
species.description: The water stored inside the tuft on its head is full of nutrients. Plants that receive its water grow large.

species.name: Simipour
species.description: It prefers places with clean water. When its tuft runs low, it replenishes it by siphoning up water with its tail.

species.name: Munna
species.description: It eats dreams and releases mist. The mist is pink when it’s eating a good dream, and black when it’s eating a nightmare.

species.name: Musharna
species.description: It drowses and dreams all the time. It’s best to leave it be if it’s just woken up, as it’s a terrible grump when freshly roused from sleep.

species.name: Pidove
species.description: It’s forgetful and not very bright, but many Trainers love it anyway for its friendliness and sincerity.

species.name: Tranquill
species.description: These bright Pokémon have acute memories. Apparently delivery workers often choose them as their partners.

species.name: Unfezant
species.description: This Pokémon is intelligent and intensely proud. People will sit up and take notice if you become the Trainer of one.

species.name: Blitzle
species.description: Its mane shines when it discharges electricity. They use the frequency and rhythm of these flashes to communicate.

species.name: Zebstrika
species.description: They have lightning-like movements. When Zebstrika run at full speed, the sound of thunder reverberates.

species.name: Roggenrola
species.description: When it detects a noise, it starts to move. The energy core inside it makes this Pokémon slightly warm to the touch.

species.name: Boldore
species.description: It relies on sound in order to monitor what’s in its vicinity. When angered, it will attack without ever changing the direction it’s facing.

species.name: Gigalith
species.description: Although its energy blasts can blow away a dump truck, they have a limitation— they can only be fired when the sun is out.

species.name: Woobat
species.description: It emits ultrasonic waves as it flutters about, searching for its prey—bug Pokémon.

species.name: Swoobat
species.description: The auspicious shape of this Pokémon’s nose apparently led some regions to consider Swoobat a symbol of good luck.

species.name: Drilbur
species.description: It’s a digger, using its claws to burrow through the ground. It causes damage to vegetable crops, so many farmers have little love for it.

species.name: Excadrill
species.description: Known as the Drill King, this Pokémon can tunnel through the terrain at speeds of over 90 mph.

species.name: Audino
species.description: This Pokémon has a kind heart. By touching with its feelers, Audino can gauge other creatures’ feelings and physical conditions.

species.name: Timburr
species.description: Timburr that have started carrying logs that are about three times their size are nearly ready to evolve.

species.name: Gurdurr
species.description: Gurdurr excels at demolition—construction is not its forte. In any case, there’s skill in the way this Pokémon wields its metal beam.

species.name: Conkeldurr
species.description: When going all out, this Pokémon throws aside its concrete pillars and leaps at opponents to pummel them with its fists.

species.name: Tympole
species.description: It uses sound waves to communicate with others of its kind. People and other Pokémon species can’t hear its cries of warning.

species.name: Palpitoad
species.description: On occasion, their cries are sublimely pleasing to the ear. Palpitoad with larger lumps on their bodies can sing with a wider range of sounds.

species.name: Seismitoad
species.description: This Pokémon is popular among the elderly, who say the vibrations of its lumps are great for massages.

species.name: Throh
species.description: They train in groups of five. Any member that can’t keep up will discard its belt and leave the group.

species.name: Sawk
species.description: The karate chops of a Sawk that’s trained itself to the limit can cleave the ocean itself.

species.name: Sewaddle
species.description: Since this Pokémon makes its own clothes out of leaves, it is a popular mascot for fashion designers.

species.name: Swadloon
species.description: It protects itself from the cold by wrapping up in leaves. It stays on the move, eating leaves in forests.

species.name: Leavanny
species.description: It keeps its eggs warm with heat from fermenting leaves. It also uses leaves to make warm wrappings for Sewaddle.

species.name: Venipede
species.description: Its fangs are highly venomous. If this Pokémon finds prey it thinks it can eat, it leaps for them without any thought of how things might turn out.

species.name: Whirlipede
species.description: Whirlipede protects itself with a sturdy shell and poisonous spikes while it stores up the energy it’ll need for evolution.

species.name: Scolipede
species.description: Scolipede engage in fierce territorial battles with Centiskorch. At the end of one of these battles, the victor makes a meal of the loser.

species.name: Cottonee
species.description: Weaving together the cotton of both Cottonee and Eldegoss produces exquisite cloth that’s highly prized by many luxury brands.

species.name: Whimsicott
species.description: As long as this Pokémon bathes in sunlight, its cotton keeps growing. If too much cotton fluff builds up, Whimsicott tears it off and scatters it.

species.name: Petilil
species.description: The leaves on its head are highly valued for medicinal purposes. Dry the leaves in the sun, boil them, and then drink the bitter decoction for remarkably effective relief from fatigue.

species.name: Lilligant
species.description: I suspect that its well-developed legs are the result of a life spent on mountains covered in deep snow. The scent it exudes from its flower crown heartens those in proximity.

species.name: Basculin
species.description: Though it differs from other Basculin in several respects, including demeanor—this one is gentle—I have categorized it as a regional form given the vast array of shared qualities.

species.name: Sandile
species.description: Sandile is small, but its legs and lower body are powerful. Pushing sand aside as it goes, Sandile moves through the desert as if it’s swimming.

species.name: Krokorok
species.description: Although this Pokémon has specialized eyes that allow it to see in the dark, Krokorok won’t move much at night—the desert gets cold after sunset.

species.name: Krookodile
species.description: While terribly aggressive, Krookodile also has the patience to stay hidden under sand for days, lying in wait for prey.

species.name: Darumaka
species.description: This popular symbol of good fortune will never fall over in its sleep, no matter how it’s pushed or pulled.

species.name: Darmanitan
species.description: This Pokémon’s power level rises along with the temperature of its fire, which can reach 2,500 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Maractus
species.description: Once each year, this Pokémon scatters its seeds. They’re jam-packed with nutrients, making them a precious food source out in the desert.

species.name: Dwebble
species.description: It first tries to find a rock to live in, but if there are no suitable rocks to be found, Dwebble may move in to the ports of a Hippowdon.

species.name: Crustle
species.description: Its thick claws are its greatest weapons. They’re mighty enough to crack Rhyperior’s carapace.

species.name: Scraggy
species.description: It protects itself with its durable skin. It’s thought that this Pokémon will evolve once its skin has completely stretched out.

species.name: Scrafty
species.description: While mostly known for having the temperament of an aggressive ruffian, this Pokémon takes very good care of its family, friends, and territory.

species.name: Sigilyph
species.description: A discovery was made in the desert where Sigilyph fly. The ruins of what may have been an ancient city were found beneath the sands.

species.name: Yamask
species.description: The spirit of a person from a bygone age became this Pokémon. It rambles through ruins, searching for someone who knows its face.

species.name: Cofagrigus
species.description: There are many depictions of Cofagrigus decorating ancient tombs. They’re symbols of the wealth that kings of bygone eras had.

species.name: Tirtouga
species.description: Tirtouga is considered to be the ancestor of many turtle Pokémon. It was restored to life from a fossil.

species.name: Carracosta
species.description: This Pokémon emerges from the water in search of prey despite the fact that it moves more slowly on land.

species.name: Archen
species.description: Archen is said to be the ancestor of bird Pokémon. It lived in treetops, eating berries and bug Pokémon.

species.name: Archeops
species.description: Though capable of flight, Archeops was apparently better at hunting on the ground.

species.name: Trubbish
species.description: This Pokémon was born from a bag stuffed with trash. Galarian Weezing relish the fumes belched by Trubbish.

species.name: Garbodor
species.description: The toxic liquid it launches from its right arm is so virulent that it can kill a weakened creature instantly.

species.name: Zorua
species.description: A once-departed soul, returned to life in Hisui. Derives power from resentment, which rises as energy atop its head and takes on the forms of foes. In this way, Zorua vents lingering malice.

species.name: Zoroark
species.description: With its disheveled white fur, it looks like an embodiment of death. Heedless of its own safety, Zoroark attacks its nemeses with a bitter energy so intense, it lacerates Zoroark's own body.

species.name: Minccino
species.description: They pet each other with their tails as a form of greeting. Of the two, the one whose tail is fluffier is a bit more boastful.

species.name: Cinccino
species.description: A special oil that seeps through their fur helps them avoid attacks. The oil fetches a high price at market.

species.name: Gothita
species.description: Even when nobody seems to be around, Gothita can still be heard making a muted cry. Many believe it’s speaking to something only it can see.

species.name: Gothorita
species.description: On nights when the stars shine, this Pokémon’s psychic power is at its strongest. It’s unknown just what link Gothorita has to the greater universe.

species.name: Gothitelle
species.description: A criminal who was shown his fate by a Gothitelle went missing that same day and was never seen again.

species.name: Solosis
species.description: Many say that the special liquid covering this Pokémon’s body would allow it to survive in the vacuum of space.

species.name: Duosion
species.description: Its brain has split into two, and the two halves rarely think alike. Its actions are utterly unpredictable.

species.name: Reuniclus
species.description: It’s said that drinking the liquid surrounding Reuniclus grants wisdom. Problem is, the liquid is highly toxic to anything besides Reuniclus itself.

species.name: Ducklett
species.description: They are better at swimming than flying, and they happily eat their favorite food, peat moss, as they dive underwater.

species.name: Swanna
species.description: Swanna start to dance at dusk. The one dancing in the middle is the leader of the flock.

species.name: Vanillite
species.description: Supposedly, this Pokémon was born from an icicle. It spews out freezing air at −58 degrees Fahrenheit to make itself more comfortable.

species.name: Vanillish
species.description: It blasts enemies with cold air reaching −148 degrees Fahrenheit, freezing them solid. But it spares their lives afterward—it’s a kind Pokémon.

species.name: Vanilluxe
species.description: People believe this Pokémon formed when two Vanillish stuck together. Its body temperature is roughly 21 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Deerling
species.description: The turning of the seasons changes the color and scent of this Pokémon’s fur. People use it to mark the seasons.

species.name: Sawsbuck
species.description: They migrate according to the seasons, so some people call Sawsbuck the harbingers of spring.

species.name: Emolga
species.description: This Pokémon absolutely loves sweet berries. Sometimes it stuffs its cheeks full of so much food that it can’t fly properly.

species.name: Karrablast
species.description: It spits a liquid from its mouth to melt through Shelmet’s shell. Karrablast doesn’t eat the shell— it eats only the contents.

species.name: Escavalier
species.description: It charges its enemies, lances at the ready. An image of one of its duels is captured in a famous painting of Escavalier clashing with Sirfetch’d.

species.name: Foongus
species.description: The spores released from this Pokémon’s hands are highly poisonous, but when thoroughly dried, the spores can be used as stomach medicine.

species.name: Amoonguss
species.description: Amoonguss generally doesn’t move much. It tends to stand still near Poké Balls that have been dropped on the ground.

species.name: Frillish
species.description: Legend has it that the residents of a sunken ancient city changed into these Pokémon.

species.name: Jellicent
species.description: Whenever a full moon hangs in the night sky, schools of Jellicent gather near the surface of the sea, waiting for their prey to appear.

species.name: Alomomola
species.description: The reason it helps Pokémon in a weakened condition is that any Pokémon coming after them may also attack Alomomola.

species.name: Joltik
species.description: Joltik latch on to other Pokémon and suck out static electricity. They’re often found sticking to Yamper’s hindquarters.

species.name: Galvantula
species.description: It lays traps of electrified threads near the nests of bird Pokémon, aiming to snare chicks that are not yet good at flying.

species.name: Ferroseed
species.description: Mossy caves are their preferred dwellings. Enzymes contained in mosses help Ferroseed’s spikes grow big and strong.

species.name: Ferrothorn
species.description: Its spikes are harder than steel. This Pokémon crawls across rock walls by stabbing the spikes on its feelers into the stone.

species.name: Klink
species.description: It’s suspected that Klink were the inspiration behind ancient people’s invention of the first gears.

species.name: Klang
species.description: Many companies in the Galar region choose Klang as their logo. This Pokémon is considered the symbol of industrial technology.

species.name: Klinklang
species.description: The three gears that compose this Pokémon spin at high speed. Its new spiked gear isn’t a living creature.

species.name: Tynamo
species.description: One alone can emit only a trickle of electricity, so a group of them gathers to unleash a powerful electric shock.

species.name: Eelektrik
species.description: These Pokémon have a big appetite. When they spot their prey, they attack it and paralyze it with electricity.

species.name: Eelektross
species.description: They crawl out of the ocean using their arms. They will attack prey on shore and immediately drag it into the ocean.

species.name: Elgyem
species.description: This Pokémon was discovered about 50 years ago. Its highly developed brain enables it to exert its psychic powers.

species.name: Beheeyem
species.description: Sometimes found drifting above wheat fields, this Pokémon can control the memories of its opponents.

species.name: Litwick
species.description: The younger the life this Pokémon absorbs, the brighter and eerier the flame on its head burns.

species.name: Lampent
species.description: It lurks in cities, pretending to be a lamp. Once it finds someone whose death is near, it will trail quietly after them.

species.name: Chandelure
species.description: In homes illuminated by Chandelure instead of lights, funerals were a constant occurrence— or so it’s said.

species.name: Axew
species.description: They play with each other by knocking their large tusks together. Their tusks break sometimes, but they grow back so quickly that it isn’t a concern.

species.name: Fraxure
species.description: Its skin is as hard as a suit of armor. Fraxure’s favorite strategy is to tackle its opponents, stabbing them with its tusks at the same time.

species.name: Haxorus
species.description: While usually kindhearted, it can be terrifying if angered. Tusks that can slice through steel beams are how Haxorus deals with its adversaries.

species.name: Cubchoo
species.description: It sniffles before performing a move, using its frosty snot to provide an icy element to any move that needs it.

species.name: Beartic
species.description: It swims energetically through frigid seas. When it gets tired, it freezes the seawater with its breath so it can rest on the ice.

species.name: Cryogonal
species.description: When the weather gets hot, these Pokémon turn into water vapor. Cryogonal are almost never seen during summer.

species.name: Shelmet
species.description: It has a strange physiology that responds to electricity. When together with Karrablast, Shelmet evolves for some reason.

species.name: Accelgor
species.description: Discarding its shell made it nimble. To keep itself from dehydrating, it wraps its body in bands of membrane.

species.name: Stunfisk
species.description: For some reason, this Pokémon smiles slightly when it emits a strong electric current from the yellow markings on its body.

species.name: Mienfoo
species.description: Though small, Mienfoo’s temperament is fierce. Any creature that approaches Mienfoo carelessly will be greeted with a flurry of graceful attacks.

species.name: Mienshao
species.description: Delivered at blinding speeds, kicks from this Pokémon can shatter massive boulders into tiny pieces.

species.name: Druddigon
species.description: Druddigon are vicious and cunning. They take up residence in nests dug out by other Pokémon, treating the stolen nests as their own lairs.

species.name: Golett
species.description: This Pokémon was created from clay. It received orders from its master many thousands of years ago, and it still follows those orders to this day.

species.name: Golurk
species.description: There’s a theory that inside Golurk is a perpetual motion machine that produces limitless energy, but this belief hasn’t been proven.

species.name: Pawniard
species.description: A pack of these Pokémon forms to serve a Bisharp boss. Each Pawniard trains diligently, dreaming of one day taking the lead.

species.name: Bisharp
species.description: Violent conflicts erupt between Bisharp and Fraxure over places where sharpening stones can be found.

species.name: Bouffalant
species.description: These Pokémon live in herds of about 20 individuals. Bouffalant that betray the herd will lose the hair on their heads for some reason.

species.name: Rufflet
species.description: Its chick-like looks belie its hotheadedness. It challenges its parents at every opportunity, desperate to prove its strength.

species.name: Braviary
species.description: Screaming a bloodcurdling battle cry, this huge and ferocious bird Pokémon goes out on the hunt. It blasts lakes with shock waves, then scoops up any prey that float to the water's surface.

species.name: Vullaby
species.description: Vullaby grow quickly. Bones that have gotten too small for older Vullaby to wear often get passed down to younger ones in the nest.

species.name: Mandibuzz
species.description: They adorn themselves with bones. There seem to be fashion trends among them, as different bones come into and fall out of popularity.

species.name: Heatmor
species.description: A flame serves as its tongue, melting through the hard shell of Durant so that Heatmor can devour their insides.

species.name: Durant
species.description: With their large mandibles, these Pokémon can crunch their way through rock. They work together to protect their eggs from Sandaconda.

species.name: Deino
species.description: Because it can’t see, this Pokémon is constantly biting at everything it touches, trying to keep track of its surroundings.

species.name: Zweilous
species.description: Their two heads will fight each other over a single piece of food. Zweilous are covered in scars even without battling others.

species.name: Hydreigon
species.description: The three heads take turns sinking their teeth into the opponent. Their attacks won’t slow until their target goes down.

species.name: Larvesta
species.description: Larvesta’s body is warm all over. It spouts fire from the tips of its horns to intimidate predators and scare prey.

species.name: Volcarona
species.description: This Pokémon emerges from a cocoon formed of raging flames. Ancient murals depict Volcarona as a deity of fire.

species.name: Cobalion
species.description: From the moment it’s born, this Pokémon radiates the air of a leader. Its presence will calm even vicious foes.

species.name: Terrakion
species.description: In Unovan legend, Terrakion battled against humans in an effort to protect other Pokémon.

species.name: Virizion
species.description: It darts around opponents with a flurry of quick movements, slicing them up with its horns.

species.name: Tornadus
species.description: This storm-stirring Pokémon is said to cause the seasons to turn by whipping up the air. I suspect its humanlike form to be a false one.

species.name: Thundurus
species.description: They say this wielder of electricity has waged war with its nemesis, Tornadus, since time immemorial. The lightning bolts it hurls pierce the very earth and enrich the soil.

species.name: Reshiram
species.description: According to myth, if people ignore truth and let themselves become consumed by greed, Reshiram will arrive to burn their kingdoms down.

species.name: Zekrom
species.description: Mythology tells us that if people lose the righteousness in their hearts, their kingdoms will be razed by Zekrom’s lightning.

species.name: Landorus
species.description: When the incarnations of wind and of lightning clash, Landorus arrives to quell the conflict. After the tempests and thunderbolts abate, the land is sure to be blessed with bountiful harvests.

species.name: Kyurem
species.description: It appears that this Pokémon uses its powers over ice to freeze its own body in order to stabilize its cellular structure.

species.name: Keldeo
species.description: They say that Keldeo must survive harsh battles and fully develop the horn on its forehead before this Pokémon’s true power will awaken.

species.name: Meloetta
species.description: Its melodies are sung with a special vocalization method that can control the feelings of those who hear it.

species.name: Genesect
species.description: This Pokémon existed 300 million years ago. Team Plasma altered it and attached a cannon to its back.

species.name: Chespin
species.description: Such a thick shell of wood covers its head and back that even a direct hit from a truck wouldn’t faze it.

species.name: Quilladin
species.description: They strengthen their lower bodies by running into one another. They are very kind and won’t start fights.

species.name: Chesnaught
species.description: When it takes a defensive posture with its fists guarding its face, it could withstand a bomb blast.

species.name: Fennekin
species.description: As it walks, it munches on a twig in place of a snack. It intimidates opponents by puffing hot air out of its ears.

species.name: Braixen
species.description: When the twig is plucked from its tail, friction sets the twig alight. The flame is used to send signals to its allies.

species.name: Delphox
species.description: Using psychic power, it generates a fiery vortex of 5,400 degrees Fahrenheit, incinerating foes swept into this whirl of flame.

species.name: Froakie
species.description: It protects its skin by covering its body in delicate bubbles. Beneath its happy-go-lucky air, it keeps a watchful eye on its surroundings.

species.name: Frogadier
species.description: Its swiftness is unparalleled. It can scale a tower of more than 2,000 feet in a minute’s time.

species.name: Greninja
species.description: It appears and vanishes with a ninja’s grace. It toys with its enemies using swift movements, while slicing them with throwing stars of sharpest water.

species.name: Bunnelby
species.description: It’s very sensitive to danger. The sound of Corviknight’s flapping will have Bunnelby digging a hole to hide underground in moments.

species.name: Diggersby
species.description: The fur on its belly retains heat exceptionally well. People used to make heavy winter clothing from fur shed by this Pokémon.

species.name: Fletchling
species.description: When this Pokémon gets excited, its body temperature increases sharply. If you touch a Fletchling with bare hands, you might get burned.

species.name: Fletchinder
species.description: Fletchinder are exceedingly territorial and aggressive. These Pokémon fight among themselves over feeding grounds.

species.name: Talonflame
species.description: Talonflame dives toward prey at speeds of up to 310 mph and assaults them with powerful kicks, giving the prey no chance to escape.

species.name: Scatterbug
species.description: The powder that covers its body regulates its temperature, so it can live in any region or climate.

species.name: Spewpa
species.description: The beaks of bird Pokémon can’t begin to scratch its stalwart body. To defend itself, it spews powder.

species.name: Vivillon
species.description: The patterns on this Pokémon’s wings depend on the climate and topography of its habitat. It scatters colorful scales.

species.name: Litleo
species.description: This hot-blooded Pokémon is filled with curiosity. When it gets angry or starts fighting, its short mane gets hot.

species.name: Pyroar
species.description: The temperature of its breath is over 10,000 degrees Fahrenheit, but Pyroar doesn’t use it on its prey. This Pokémon prefers to eat raw meat.

species.name: Flabébé
species.description: Flabébé wears a crown made from pollen it’s collected from its flower. The crown has hidden healing properties.

species.name: Floette
species.description: It gives its own power to flowers, pouring its heart into caring for them. Floette never forgives anyone who messes up a flower bed.

species.name: Florges
species.description: Its life can span several hundred years. It’s said to devote its entire life to protecting gardens.

species.name: Skiddo
species.description: If it has sunshine and water, it doesn’t need to eat, because it can generate energy from the leaves on its back.

species.name: Gogoat
species.description: They inhabit mountainous regions. The leader of the herd is decided by a battle of clashing horns.

species.name: Pancham
species.description: Wanting to make sure it’s taken seriously, Pancham’s always giving others a glare. But if it’s not focusing, it ends up smiling.

species.name: Pangoro
species.description: Using its leaf, Pangoro can predict the moves of its opponents. It strikes with punches that can turn a dump truck into scrap with just one hit.

species.name: Furfrou
species.description: Left alone, its fur will grow longer and longer, but it will only allow someone it trusts to cut it.

species.name: Espurr
species.description: There’s enough psychic power in Espurr to send a wrestler flying, but because this power can’t be controlled, Espurr finds it troublesome.

species.name: Meowstic
species.description: The defensive instinct of the males is strong. It’s when they’re protecting themselves or their partners that they unleash their full power.

species.name: Honedge
species.description: The blue eye on the sword’s handguard is the true body of Honedge. With its old cloth, it drains people’s lives away.

species.name: Doublade
species.description: The two swords employ a strategy of rapidly alternating between offense and defense to bring down their prey.

species.name: Aegislash
species.description: Its potent spectral powers allow it to manipulate others. It once used its powers to force people and Pokémon to build a kingdom to its liking.

species.name: Spritzee
species.description: The scent its body gives off enraptures those who smell it. Noble ladies had no shortage of love for Spritzee.

species.name: Aromatisse
species.description: The scents Aromatisse can produce range from sweet smells that bolster allies to foul smells that sap an opponent’s will to fight.

species.name: Swirlix
species.description: The sweet smell of cotton candy perfumes Swirlix’s fluffy fur. This Pokémon spits out sticky string to tangle up its enemies.

species.name: Slurpuff
species.description: Slurpuff’s fur contains a lot of air, making it soft to the touch and lighter than it looks.

species.name: Inkay
species.description: By exposing foes to the blinking of its luminescent spots, Inkay demoralizes them, and then it seizes the chance to flee.

species.name: Malamar
species.description: It’s said that Malamar’s hypnotic powers played a role in certain history-changing events.

species.name: Binacle
species.description: If the two don’t work well together, both their offense and defense fall apart. Without good teamwork, they won’t survive.

species.name: Barbaracle
species.description: Having an eye on each palm allows it to keep watch in all directions. In a pinch, its limbs start to act on their own to ensure the enemy’s defeat.

species.name: Skrelp
species.description: Skrelp looks like a piece of rotten seaweed, so it can blend in with seaweed drifting on the ocean and avoid being detected by enemies.

species.name: Dragalge
species.description: Dragalge generates dragon energy by sticking the plume on its head out above the ocean’s surface and bathing it in sunlight.

species.name: Clauncher
species.description: By detonating gas that accumulates in its right claw, this Pokémon launches water like a bullet. This is how Clauncher defeats its enemies.

species.name: Clawitzer
species.description: Clawitzer’s right arm is a cannon that launches projectiles made of seawater. Shots from a Clawitzer’s cannon arm can sink a tanker.

species.name: Helioptile
species.description: The sun powers this Pokémon’s electricity generation. Interruption of that process stresses Helioptile to the point of weakness.

species.name: Heliolisk
species.description: One Heliolisk basking in the sun with its frill outspread is all it would take to produce enough electricity to power a city.

species.name: Tyrunt
species.description: This Pokémon is selfish and likes to be pampered. It can also inflict grievous wounds on its Trainer just by playing around.

species.name: Tyrantrum
species.description: A single bite of Tyrantrum’s massive jaws will demolish a car. This Pokémon was the king of the ancient world.

species.name: Amaura
species.description: Amaura is an ancient Pokémon that has gone extinct. Specimens of this species can sometimes be found frozen in ice.

species.name: Aurorus
species.description: When gripped by rage, Aurorus will emanate freezing air, covering everything around it in ice.

species.name: Sylveon
species.description: It emits a soothing aura from its ribbon-shaped organs. It wraps these appendages around quarrelers to instantly restore calm to the situation.

species.name: Hawlucha
species.description: It always strikes a pose before going for its finishing move. Sometimes opponents take advantage of that time to counterattack.

species.name: Dedenne
species.description: Since Dedenne can’t generate much electricity on its own, it steals electricity from outlets or other electric Pokémon.

species.name: Carbink
species.description: It’s said that somewhere in the world, there’s a mineral vein housing a large pack of slumbering Carbink. It’s also said that this pack has a queen.

species.name: Goomy
species.description: Goomy hides away in the shade of trees, where it's nice and humid. If the slime coating its body dries out, the Pokémon instantly becomes lethargic.

species.name: Sliggoo
species.description: A creature given to melancholy. I suspect its metallic shell developed as a result of the mucus on its skin reacting with the iron in Hisui's water.

species.name: Goodra
species.description: Able to freely control the hardness of its metallic shell. It loathes solitude and is extremely clingy—it will fume and run riot if those dearest to it ever leave its side.

species.name: Klefki
species.description: Klefki sucks in metal ions with the horn topping its head. It seems this Pokémon loves keys so much that its head needed to look like one, too.

species.name: Phantump
species.description: With a voice like a human child’s, it cries out to lure adults deep into the forest, getting them lost among the trees.

species.name: Trevenant
species.description: Small roots that extend from the tips of this Pokémon’s feet can tie into the trees of the forest and give Trevenant control over them.

species.name: Pumpkaboo
species.description: The light that streams out from the holes in the pumpkin can hypnotize and control the people and Pokémon that see it.

species.name: Gourgeist
species.description: In the darkness of a new-moon night, Gourgeist will come knocking. Whoever answers the door will be swept off to the afterlife.

species.name: Bergmite
species.description: Lives on mountains blanketed in perennial snow. It freezes water vapor in the air to make the ice helmet that it dons for defense.

species.name: Avalugg
species.description: The armor of ice covering its lower jaw puts steel to shame and can shatter rocks with ease. This Pokémon barrels along steep mountain paths, cleaving through the deep snow.

species.name: Noibat
species.description: No wavelength of sound is beyond Noibat’s ability to produce. The ultrasonic waves it generates can overcome much larger Pokémon.

species.name: Noivern
species.description: Flying through the darkness, it weakens enemies with ultrasonic waves that could crush stone. Its fangs finish the fight.

species.name: Xerneas
species.description: When the horns on its head shine in seven colors, it is said to be sharing everlasting life.

species.name: Yveltal
species.description: When its life comes to an end, it absorbs the life energy of every living thing and turns into a cocoon once more.

species.name: Zygarde
species.description: Some say it can change to an even more powerful form when battling those who threaten the ecosystem.

species.name: Diancie
species.description: It can instantly create many diamonds by compressing the carbon in the air between its hands.

species.name: Hoopa
species.description: It is said to be able to seize anything it desires with its six rings and six huge arms. With its power sealed, it is transformed into a much smaller form.

species.name: Volcanion
species.description: It expels its internal steam from the arms on its back. It has enough power to blow away a mountain.

species.name: Rowlet
species.description: Flies noiselessly on delicate wings. It has mastered the art of deftly launching dagger-sharp feathers from those same wings.

species.name: Dartrix
species.description: Regularly basks in sunlight to gather power—presumably due to the frigid climate. Nonetheless, the edges of the blade quills set into its wings are keen as ever.

species.name: Decidueye
species.description: The air stored inside the rachises of Decidueye's feathers insulates the Pokémon against Hisui's extreme cold. This is firm proof that evolution can be influenced by environment.

species.name: Litten
species.description: Trying to pet Litten before it trusts you will result in a nasty scratch from its sharp claws. Be careful.

species.name: Torracat
species.description: When facing a powerful enemy, Torracat’s fighting spirit gets pumped up, and its fire bell blazes hotter.

species.name: Incineroar
species.description: Incineroar’s rough and aggressive behavior is its most notable trait, but the way it helps out small Pokémon shows that it has a kind side as well.

species.name: Popplio
species.description: If Popplio want to create big, powerful balloons, they must be persistent. It takes daily practice for these Pokémon to develop their skills.

species.name: Brionne
species.description: On nights when the sea is calm, Brionne dance with one another to the singing of the Primarina that’s leading them.

species.name: Primarina
species.description: For Primarina, every battle’s a stage. Its singing and the dancing of its balloons will mesmerize the audience.

species.name: Pikipek
species.description: It may look spindly, but its neck muscles are heavy-duty. It can peck at a tree 16 times per second!

species.name: Trumbeak
species.description: From its mouth, it fires the seeds of berries it has eaten. The scattered seeds give rise to new plants.

species.name: Toucannon
species.description: Known for forming harmonious couples, this Pokémon is brought to wedding ceremonies as a good luck charm.

species.name: Yungoos
species.description: Although it will eat anything, it prefers fresh living things, so it marches down streets in search of prey.

species.name: Gumshoos
species.description: Patient by nature, this Pokémon loses control of itself and pounces when it spots its favorite meal—Rattata!

species.name: Grubbin
species.description: It uses its big jaws to dig nests into the forest floor, and it loves to feed on sweet tree sap.

species.name: Charjabug
species.description: Its digestive processes convert the leaves it eats into electricity. An electric sac in its belly stores the electricity for later use.

species.name: Vikavolt
species.description: If it carries a Charjabug to use as a spare battery, a flying Vikavolt can rapidly fire high-powered beams of electricity.

species.name: Crabrawler
species.description: Crabrawler has been known to mistake Exeggutor for a coconut tree and climb it. The enraged Exeggutor shakes it off and stomps it.

species.name: Crabominable
species.description: Before it stops to think, it just starts pummeling. There are records of its turning back avalanches with a flurry of punches.

species.name: Oricorio
species.description: This Oricorio has drunk red nectar. If its Trainer gives the wrong order, this passionate Pokémon becomes fiercely angry.

species.name: Cutiefly
species.description: An opponent’s aura can tell Cutiefly what that opponent’s next move will be. Then Cutiefly can glide around the attack and strike back.

species.name: Ribombee
species.description: Ribombee absolutely hate getting wet or rained on. In the cloudy Galar region, they are very seldom seen.

species.name: Rockruff
species.description: This Pokémon intimidates opponents by striking the ground with the rocks on its neck. The moment an opponent flinches, Rockruff attacks.

species.name: Lycanroc
species.description: With swift movements, this Pokémon gradually backs its prey into a corner. Lycanroc’s fangs are always aimed toward opponents’ weak spots.

species.name: Wishiwashi
species.description: When it senses danger, its eyes tear up. The sparkle of its tears signals other Wishiwashi to gather.

species.name: Mareanie
species.description: Unlike their Alolan counterparts, the Mareanie of the Galar region have not yet figured out that the branches of Corsola are delicious.

species.name: Toxapex
species.description: Within the poison sac in its body is a poison so toxic that Pokémon as large as Wailord will still be suffering three days after it first takes effect.

species.name: Mudbray
species.description: It eats dirt to create mud and smears this mud all over its feet, giving them the grip needed to walk on rough terrain without slipping.

species.name: Mudsdale
species.description: Mudsdale has so much stamina that it could carry over 10 tons across the Galar region without rest or sleep.

species.name: Dewpider
species.description: Dewpider normally lives underwater. When it comes onto land in search of food, it takes water with it in the form of a bubble on its head.

species.name: Araquanid
species.description: It acts as a caretaker for Dewpider, putting them inside its bubble and letting them eat any leftover food.

species.name: Fomantis
species.description: During the day, Fomantis basks in sunlight and sleeps peacefully. It wakes and moves around at night.

species.name: Lurantis
species.description: The petals on this Pokémon’s arms are thin and super sharp, and they can fire laser beams if Lurantis gathers light first.

species.name: Morelull
species.description: Morelull live in forests that stay dark even during the day. They scatter flickering spores that put enemies to sleep.

species.name: Shiinotic
species.description: If you see a light deep in a forest at night, don’t go near. Shiinotic will make you fall fast asleep.

species.name: Salandit
species.description: This sneaky Pokémon will slink behind its prey and immobilize it with poisonous gas before the prey even realizes Salandit is there.

species.name: Salazzle
species.description: The winner of competitions between Salazzle is decided by which one has the most male Salandit with it.

species.name: Stufful
species.description: The way it protects itself by flailing its arms may be an adorable sight, but stay well away. This is flailing that can snap thick tree trunks.

species.name: Bewear
species.description: The moves it uses to take down its prey would make a martial artist jealous. It tucks subdued prey under its arms to carry them to its nest.

species.name: Bounsweet
species.description: When under attack, it secretes a sweet and delicious sweat. The scent only calls more enemies to it.

species.name: Steenee
species.description: Any Corvisquire that pecks at this Pokémon will be greeted with a smack from its sepals followed by a sharp kick.

species.name: Tsareena
species.description: A kick from the hardened tips of this Pokémon’s legs leaves a wound in the opponent’s body and soul that will never heal.

species.name: Comfey
species.description: These Pokémon smell very nice. All Comfey wear different flowers, so each of these Pokémon has its own individual scent.

species.name: Oranguru
species.description: It knows the forest inside and out. If it comes across a wounded Pokémon, Oranguru will gather medicinal herbs to treat it.

species.name: Passimian
species.description: Passimian live in groups of about 20, with each member performing an assigned role. Through cooperation, the group survives.

species.name: Wimpod
species.description: Wimpod gather in swarms, constantly on the lookout for danger. They scatter the moment they detect an enemy’s presence.

species.name: Golisopod
species.description: They live in sunken ships or in holes in the seabed. When Golisopod and Grapploct battle, the loser becomes the winner’s meal.

species.name: Sandygast
species.description: Sandygast mainly inhabits beaches. It takes control of anyone who puts their hand into its mouth, forcing them to make its body bigger.

species.name: Palossand
species.description: This Pokémon lives on beaches, but it hates water. Palossand can’t maintain its castle-like shape if it gets drenched by a heavy rain.

species.name: Pyukumuku
species.description: It’s covered in a slime that keeps its skin moist, allowing it to stay on land for days without drying up.

species.name: Type: Null
species.description: It was modeled after a mighty Pokémon of myth. The mask placed upon it limits its power in order to keep it under control.

species.name: Silvally
species.description: The final factor needed to release this Pokémon’s true power was a strong bond with a Trainer it trusts.

species.name: Minior
species.description: Although its outer shell is uncommonly durable, the shock of falling to the ground smashes the shell to smithereens.

species.name: Komala
species.description: It remains asleep from birth to death as a result of the sedative properties of the leaves that form its diet.

species.name: Turtonator
species.description: Eating sulfur in its volcanic habitat is what causes explosive compounds to develop in its shell. Its droppings are also dangerously explosive.

species.name: Togedemaru
species.description: When it’s in trouble, it curls up into a ball, makes its fur spikes stand on end, and then discharges electricity indiscriminately.

species.name: Mimikyu
species.description: There was a scientist who peeked under Mimikyu’s old rag in the name of research. The scientist died of a mysterious disease.

species.name: Bruxish
species.description: Its skin is thick enough to fend off Mareanie’s spikes. With its robust teeth, Bruxish crunches up the spikes and eats them.

species.name: Drampa
species.description: Drampa is a kind and friendly Pokémon—up until it’s angered. When that happens, it stirs up a gale and flattens everything around.

species.name: Dhelmise
species.description: After lowering its anchor, it waits for its prey. It catches large Wailord and drains their life-force.

species.name: Jangmo-o
species.description: Jangmo-o strikes its scales to communicate with others of its kind. Its scales are actually fur that’s become as hard as metal.

species.name: Hakamo-o
species.description: Before attacking its enemies, it clashes its scales together and roars. Its sharp claws shred the opposition.

species.name: Kommo-o
species.description: Certain ruins have paintings of ancient warriors wearing armor made of Kommo-o scales.

species.name: Tapu Koko
species.description: The lightning-wielding guardian deity of Melemele, Tapu Koko is brimming with curiosity and appears before people from time to time.

species.name: Tapu Lele
species.description: Although called a guardian deity, Tapu Lele is devoid of guilt about its cruel disposition and can be described as nature incarnate.

species.name: Tapu Bulu
species.description: It makes ringing sounds with its tail to let others know where it is, avoiding unneeded conflicts. This guardian deity of Ula’ula controls plants.

species.name: Tapu Fini
species.description: Although it’s called a guardian deity, terrible calamities sometimes befall those who recklessly approach Tapu Fini.

species.name: Cosmog
species.description: Cosmog is very curious but not very cautious, often placing itself in danger. If things start to look dicey, it teleports away.

species.name: Cosmoem
species.description: It sucks in dust from the air at an astounding rate, frantically building up energy within its core as preparation for evolution.

species.name: Solgaleo
species.description: Solgaleo was once known as the Beast That Devours the Sun. Energy in the form of light radiates boundlessly from it.

species.name: Lunala
species.description: It steals the light from its surroundings and then becomes the full moon, showering its own light across the night sky.

species.name: Nihilego
species.description: It appeared in this world from an Ultra Wormhole. Nihilego appears to be a parasite that lives by feeding on people and Pokémon.

species.name: Buzzwole
species.description: Buzzwole goes around showing off its abnormally swollen muscles. It is one kind of Ultra Beast.

species.name: Pheromosa
species.description: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

species.name: Xurkitree
species.description: They’ve been dubbed Ultra Beasts. Some of them stand unmoving, like trees, with their arms and legs stuck into the ground.

species.name: Celesteela
species.description: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

species.name: Kartana
species.description: Although it’s alien to this world and a danger here, it’s apparently a common organism in the world where it normally lives.

species.name: Guzzlord
species.description: An unknown life-form called a UB. It may be constantly hungry—it is certainly always devouring something.

species.name: Necrozma
species.description: It needs light to survive, and it goes on a rampage seeking it out. Its laser beams will cut anything to pieces.

species.name: Magearna
species.description: Built roughly 500 years ago by a scientist, the part called the Soul-Heart is the actual life-form.

species.name: Marshadow
species.description: This Pokémon can conceal itself in any shadow, so it went undiscovered for a long time.

species.name: Poipole
species.description: An Ultra Beast that lives in a different world, it cackles wildly as it sprays its opponents with poison from the needles on its head.

species.name: Naganadel
species.description: One kind of Ultra Beast, it fires a glowing, venomous liquid from its needles. This liquid is also immensely adhesive.

species.name: Stakataka
species.description: When stone walls started moving and attacking, the brute’s true identity was this mysterious life-form, which brings to mind an Ultra Beast.

species.name: Blacephalon
species.description: A UB that appeared from an Ultra Wormhole, it causes explosions, then takes advantage of opponents’ surprise to rob them of their vitality.

species.name: Zeraora
species.description: Electricity sparks from the pads on its limbs. Wherever Zeraora runs, lightning flashes and thunder echoes.

species.name: Meltan
species.description: They live as a group, but when the time comes, one strong Meltan will absorb all the others and evolve.

species.name: Melmetal
species.description: Centrifugal force is behind the punches of Melmetal’s heavy hex-nut arms. Melmetal is said to deliver the strongest punches of all Pokémon.

species.name: Grookey
species.description: It attacks with rapid beats of its stick. As it strikes with amazing speed, it gets more and more pumped.

species.name: Thwackey
species.description: When it’s drumming out rapid beats in battle, it gets so caught up in the rhythm that it won’t even notice that it’s already knocked out its opponent.

species.name: Rillaboom
species.description: The one with the best drumming techniques becomes the boss of the troop. It has a gentle disposition and values harmony among its group.

species.name: Scorbunny
species.description: It has special pads on the backs of its feet, and one on its nose. Once it’s raring to fight, these pads radiate tremendous heat.

species.name: Raboot
species.description: It kicks berries right off the branches of trees and then juggles them with its feet, practicing its footwork.

species.name: Cinderace
species.description: It’s skilled at both offense and defense, and it gets pumped up when cheered on. But if it starts showboating, it could put itself in a tough spot.

species.name: Sobble
species.description: When it gets wet, its skin changes color, and this Pokémon becomes invisible as if it were camouflaged.

species.name: Drizzile
species.description: Highly intelligent but also very lazy, it keeps enemies out of its territory by laying traps everywhere.

species.name: Inteleon
species.description: Its nictitating membranes let it pick out foes’ weak points so it can precisely blast them with water that shoots from its fingertips at Mach 3.

species.name: Skwovet
species.description: It eats berries nonstop—a habit that has made it more resilient than it looks. It’ll show up on farms, searching for yet more berries.

species.name: Greedent
species.description: Common throughout the Galar region, this Pokémon has strong teeth and can chew through the toughest of berry shells.

species.name: Rookidee
species.description: Jumping nimbly about, this small-bodied Pokémon takes advantage of even the slightest opportunity to disorient larger opponents.

species.name: Corvisquire
species.description: The lessons of many harsh battles have taught it how to accurately judge an opponent’s strength.

species.name: Corviknight
species.description: With their great intellect and flying skills, these Pokémon very successfully act as the Galar region’s airborne taxi service.

species.name: Blipbug
species.description: Often found in gardens, this Pokémon has hairs on its body that it uses to assess its surroundings.

species.name: Dottler
species.description: As it grows inside its shell, it uses its psychic abilities to monitor the outside world and prepare for evolution.

species.name: Orbeetle
species.description: It emits psychic energy to observe and study what’s around it—and what’s around it can include things over six miles away.

species.name: Nickit
species.description: Cunning and cautious, this Pokémon survives by stealing food from others. It erases its tracks with swipes of its tail as it makes off with its plunder.

species.name: Thievul
species.description: With a lithe body and sharp claws, it goes around stealing food and eggs. Boltund is its natural enemy.

species.name: Gossifleur
species.description: It whirls around in the wind while singing a joyous song. This delightful display has charmed many into raising this Pokémon.

species.name: Eldegoss
species.description: The cotton on the head of this Pokémon can be spun into a glossy, gorgeous yarn—a Galar regional specialty.

species.name: Wooloo
species.description: If its fleece grows too long, Wooloo won’t be able to move. Cloth made with the wool of this Pokémon is surprisingly strong.

species.name: Dubwool
species.description: Its majestic horns are meant only to impress the opposite gender. They never see use in battle.

species.name: Chewtle
species.description: It starts off battles by attacking with its rock-hard horn, but as soon as the opponent flinches, this Pokémon bites down and never lets go.

species.name: Drednaw
species.description: This Pokémon rapidly extends its retractable neck to sink its sharp fangs into distant enemies and take them down.

species.name: Yamper
species.description: This gluttonous Pokémon only assists people with their work because it wants treats. As it runs, it crackles with electricity.

species.name: Boltund
species.description: It sends electricity through its legs to boost their strength. Running at top speed, it easily breaks 50 mph.

species.name: Rolycoly
species.description: It can race around like a unicycle, even on rough, rocky terrain. Burning coal sustains it.

species.name: Carkol
species.description: By rapidly rolling its legs, it can travel at over 18 mph. The temperature of the flames it breathes exceeds 1,800 degrees Fahrenheit.

species.name: Coalossal
species.description: While it’s engaged in battle, its mountain of coal will burn bright red, sending off sparks that scorch the surrounding area.

species.name: Applin
species.description: As soon as it’s born, it burrows into an apple. Not only does the apple serve as its food source, but the flavor of the fruit determines its evolution.

species.name: Flapple
species.description: It flies on wings of apple skin and spits a powerful acid. It can also change its shape into that of an apple.

species.name: Appletun
species.description: Its body is covered in sweet nectar, and the skin on its back is especially yummy. Children used to have it as a snack.

species.name: Silicobra
species.description: It spews sand from its nostrils. While the enemy is blinded, it burrows into the ground to hide.

species.name: Sandaconda
species.description: Its unique style of coiling allows it to blast sand out of its sand sac more efficiently.

species.name: Cramorant
species.description: This hungry Pokémon swallows Arrokuda whole. Occasionally, it makes a mistake and tries to swallow a Pokémon other than its preferred prey.

species.name: Arrokuda
species.description: After it’s eaten its fill, its movements become extremely sluggish. That’s when Cramorant swallows it up.

species.name: Barraskewda
species.description: It spins its tail fins to propel itself, surging forward at speeds of over 100 knots before ramming prey and spearing into them.

species.name: Toxel
species.description: It manipulates the chemical makeup of its poison to produce electricity. The voltage is weak, but it can cause a tingling paralysis.

species.name: Toxtricity
species.description: This short-tempered and aggressive Pokémon chugs stagnant water to absorb any toxins it might contain.

species.name: Sizzlipede
species.description: It wraps prey up with its heated body, cooking them in its coils. Once they’re well-done, it will voraciously nibble them down to the last morsel.

species.name: Centiskorch
species.description: While its burning body is already dangerous on its own, this excessively hostile Pokémon also has large and very sharp fangs.

species.name: Clobbopus
species.description: Its tentacles tear off easily, but it isn’t alarmed when that happens—it knows they’ll grow back. It’s about as smart as a three-year-old.

species.name: Grapploct
species.description: Searching for an opponent to test its skills against, it emerges onto land. Once the battle is over, it returns to the sea.

species.name: Sinistea
species.description: The teacup in which this Pokémon makes its home is a famous piece of antique tableware. Many forgeries are in circulation.

species.name: Polteageist
species.description: Leaving leftover black tea unattended is asking for this Pokémon to come along and pour itself into it, turning the tea into a new Polteageist.

species.name: Hatenna
species.description: If this Pokémon senses a strong emotion, it will run away as fast as it can. It prefers areas without people.

species.name: Hattrem
species.description: Using the braids on its head, it pummels foes to get them to quiet down. One blow from those braids would knock out a professional boxer.

species.name: Hatterene
species.description: If you’re too loud around it, you risk being torn apart by the claws on its tentacle. This Pokémon is also known as the Forest Witch.

species.name: Impidimp
species.description: It sneaks into people’s homes, stealing things and feasting on the negative energy of the frustrated occupants.

species.name: Morgrem
species.description: With sly cunning, it tries to lure people into the woods. Some believe it to have the power to make crops grow.

species.name: Grimmsnarl
species.description: Its hairs work like muscle fibers. When its hairs unfurl, they latch on to opponents, ensnaring them as tentacles would.

species.name: Obstagoon
species.description: It evolved after experiencing numerous fights. While crossing its arms, it lets out a shout that would make any opponent flinch.

species.name: Perrserker
species.description: After many battles, it evolved dangerous claws that come together to form daggers when extended.

species.name: Cursola
species.description: Be cautious of the ectoplasmic body surrounding its soul. You’ll become stiff as stone if you touch it.

species.name: Sirfetch’d
species.description: After deflecting attacks with its hard leaf shield, it strikes back with its sharp leek stalk. The leek stalk is both weapon and food.

species.name: Mr. Rime
species.description: Its amusing movements make it very popular. It releases its psychic power from the pattern on its belly.

species.name: Runerigus
species.description: Never touch its shadowlike body, or you’ll be shown the horrific memories behind the picture carved into it.

species.name: Milcery
species.description: They say that any patisserie visited by Milcery is guaranteed success and good fortune.

species.name: Alcremie
species.description: When Alcremie is content, the cream it secretes from its hands becomes sweeter and richer.

species.name: Falinks
species.description: The six of them work together as one Pokémon. Teamwork is also their battle strategy, and they constantly change their formation as they fight.

species.name: Pincurchin
species.description: It stores electricity in each spine. Even if one gets broken off, it still continues to emit electricity for at least three hours.

species.name: Snom
species.description: It eats snow that piles up on the ground. The more snow it eats, the bigger and more impressive the spikes on its back grow.

species.name: Frosmoth
species.description: It shows no mercy to any who desecrate fields and mountains. It will fly around on its icy wings, causing a blizzard to chase offenders away.

species.name: Stonjourner
species.description: Once a year, on a specific date and at a specific time, they gather out of nowhere and form up in a circle.

species.name: Eiscue
species.description: This Pokémon keeps its heat-sensitive head cool with ice. It fishes for its food, dangling its single hair into the sea to lure in prey.

species.name: Indeedee
species.description: Through its horns, it can pick up on the emotions of creatures around it. Positive emotions are the source of its strength.

species.name: Morpeko
species.description: It carries electrically roasted seeds with it as if they’re precious treasures. No matter how much it eats, it always gets hungry again in short order.

species.name: Cufant
species.description: If a job requires serious strength, this Pokémon will excel at it. Its copper body tarnishes in the rain, turning a vibrant green color.

species.name: Copperajah
species.description: These Pokémon live in herds. Their trunks have incredible grip strength, strong enough to crush giant rocks into powder.

species.name: Dracozolt
species.description: The powerful muscles in its tail generate its electricity. Compared to its lower body, its upper half is entirely too small.

species.name: Arctozolt
species.description: This Pokémon lived on prehistoric seashores and was able to preserve food with the ice on its body. It went extinct because it moved so slowly.

species.name: Dracovish
species.description: Its mighty legs are capable of running at speeds exceeding 40 mph, but this Pokémon can’t breathe unless it’s underwater.

species.name: Arctovish
species.description: The skin on its face is impervious to attack, but breathing difficulties made this Pokémon go extinct anyway.

species.name: Duraludon
species.description: The special metal that composes its body is very light, so this Pokémon has considerable agility. It lives in caves because it dislikes the rain.

species.name: Dreepy
species.description: If this weak Pokémon is by itself, a mere child could defeat it. But if Dreepy has friends to help it train, it can evolve and become much stronger.

species.name: Drakloak
species.description: Without a Dreepy to place on its head and care for, it gets so uneasy it’ll try to substitute any Pokémon it finds for the missing Dreepy.

species.name: Dragapult
species.description: Apparently the Dreepy inside Dragapult’s horns eagerly look forward to being launched out at Mach speeds.

species.name: Zacian
species.description: This Pokémon has slumbered for many years. Some say it’s Zamazenta’s elder sister—others say the two Pokémon are rivals.

species.name: Zamazenta
species.description: This Pokémon slept for aeons while in the form of a statue. It was asleep for so long, people forgot that it ever existed.

species.name: Eternatus
species.description: It was inside a meteorite that fell 20,000 years ago. There seems to be a connection between this Pokémon and the Dynamax phenomenon.

species.name: Kubfu
species.description: If Kubfu pulls the long white hair on its head, its fighting spirit heightens and power wells up from the depths of its belly.

species.name: Urshifu
species.description: Inhabiting the mountains of a distant region, this Pokémon races across sheer cliffs, training its legs and refining its moves.

species.name: Zarude
species.description: Once the vines on Zarude’s body tear off, they become nutrients in the soil. This helps the plants of the forest grow.

species.name: Regieleki
species.description: Its entire body is made up of a single organ that generates electrical energy. Regieleki is capable of creating all Galar’s electricity.
